pi_items.source: Task description (task.json:5) references course shortname HIST201; setup_task.sh (line 54) creates course with shortname='HIST201'; export_result.sh (line 38) queries mdl_course WHERE shortname='HIST201'; task description states 'World History' (HIST201) as the course name

Task description (task.json:5) references assignment 'Final Research Paper'; setup_task.sh (line 101) creates assignment with name='Final Research Paper'; export_result.sh (line 42) queries mdl_assign WHERE name='Final Research Paper'

Task description (task.json:5) references student 'Bob Brown (bbrown)'; setup_task.sh (line 67) creates user with username='bbrown', firstname='Bob', lastname='Brown'; export_result.sh (line 58) queries mdl_user WHERE username='bbrown'

Task description (task.json:5) specifies 'Change the marking workflow state for student Bob Brown (bbrown) to In review'; Moodle documentation (Lafayette College, Brookes, etc.) confirms 'In review' is valid workflow state; export_result.sh (line 65) queries SELECT workflowstate FROM mdl_assign_grades; verifier.py (line 29) retrieves expected_state from metadata; Moodle stores workflow states as lowercase identifiers ('inreview' for 'In review' state)

privileged_info_summary: The task 'manage_assignment_marking_workflow@1' operates on a Moodle LMS environment with synthetic test data. The setup_task.sh script pre-creates the course HIST201 ('World History') with a student user 'bbrown' (Bob Brown) and an assignment 'Final Research Paper' with marking workflow initially disabled (markingworkflow=0). The agent's task is to enable the marking workflow on the assignment and set the student's workflow state to 'inreview' (the 'In review' state, which indicates the grade is being double-checked). All four metadata claims are verified: course shortname is HIST201, assignment name is Final Research Paper, student username is bbrown, and the expected workflow state is inreview.
